ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'chicken soup' class (FOODON_00002372); no specific 'roasted chicken breast soup' subclass exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken soup — composite dish containing roasted chicken breast in broth
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for roasted chicken breast soup; A00LC (chicken soup) is the closest defensible category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for roasted chicken breast soup; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of roasted chicken breast soup is approximately 300 g, comprising broth, roasted chicken breast pieces, and vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: Roasted Chicken Breast Soup — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Roasted Chicken Breast Soup (cr_id G00010430). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on chicken soup / mixed protein-vegetable soup category
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Roasted Chicken Breast Soup in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 40 (low) based on the closest analogous category: mixed protein-dominant chicken soup with vegetables. The high protein content (21.15 g/100g) and moderate carbohydrate (7.34 g/100g) strongly attenuate glycaemic response. Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (40 × 7.34 × 3) / 100 = 8.8 g, classified as low GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 454 mg per 100 g exceeds the 400 mg/100 g threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to no more than 150 g per serving or request reduced-salt preparation, as a standard 300 g bowl delivers approximately 1,362 mg sodium — more than half the recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High protein (21.15 g/100g) and low estimated GI (~40) support glycaemic control despite moderate carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Roasted chicken breast soup is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in standard portions; the high protein content moderates postprandial glucose response, and the estimated glycaemic load of 8.8 per 300 g bowl is within the low-GL range, though sugar content (5.98 g/100g) should be monitored if sweet sauces are used in preparation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (454 mg/100g) and moderate cholesterol (78 mg/100g) are cardiovascular risk factors, partially offset by lean protein and low saturated fat (~2.5 g/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, this soup should be consumed in reduced portions with low-sodium preparation; the lean roasted chicken breast provides heart-healthy protein, but the sodium content of approximately 1,362 mg per standard bowl requires careful management within a daily sodium budget of 2,000 mg.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Lean roasted protein supports tissue repair but elevated sodium (454 mg/100g) may exacerbate tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Roasted chicken breast soup is broadly suitable for rheumatoid arthritis patients due to its lean protein and non-deep-fried preparation method; however, reduced-salt preparation is strongly recommended to avoid sodium-driven fluid retention and tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Roasted Chicken Breast Soup is a Western-style soup dish available in Singapore, consisting of roasted chicken breast pieces served in a savoury broth with vegetables and herbs. The chicken breast is first roasted to develop flavour and a lightly caramelised exterior before being combined with a simmered broth base, resulting in a soup that is rich in lean protein yet relatively light in texture. The broth is typically clear to lightly opaque, with a mild savoury flavour profile. Per 100 g, the dish provides 202 kcal, 21.15 g protein, 9.15 g fat, and 7.34 g carbohydrate, making it a high-protein, moderate-energy soup. Sodium content is elevated at 454 mg/100g, which is a key clinical consideration for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The estimated glycaemic index is low (~40), and a standard 300 g bowl delivers a glycaemic load of approximately 8.8, making it suitable for glycaemic control in Type 2 Diabetes management. The dish is halal-friendly and gluten-free in standard preparation. It is commonly consumed as a lunch or dinner item in Singapore's Western food stalls and casual dining settings.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for Roasted Chicken Breast Soup (cr_id G00010430) with a recorded confidence of 0.95. All core macros and key micronutrients are present. The record is classified as 'reviewed' and 'canonical' provenance in the source system.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific roasted chicken breast soup preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (G00010430) as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat estimated at 2.5 g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central reference for roasted chicken breast (SR Legacy #05064) as a supplementary estimate; this is documented as an adaptation. GI value of 40 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database protein-dominant chicken soup category; no direct measurement exists for this specific dish. All core macros (protein_g 21.15, fat_g 9.15, carbohydrate_g 7.34) are non-zero and sourced from HPB.
